avoid: Do not replace the main W @ X with a custom full GEMM; use cuBLAS SGEMM for the dominant full matrix multiply.
Do not add TORCH_CHECK validation, dtype checks, contiguity conversions, or defensive copies for this trusted-input benchmark path.
Do not spend effort tuning block size or vectorized stores before validating the simpler pure cuBLAS three-SGEMM path.
Do not hardcode hidden dimensions beyond using runtime d and fixed rank 16.
Do not promote based only on aggregate speedup when d=4096 has a clear regression.
evidence: Correctness passes for all tested d values, so the issue is performance rather than numerical failure.
Aggregate speedup is essentially neutral at 1.0019x.
Performance is shape-dependent: d=3584 improves by 1.043x and d=4608 improves by 1.037x, but d=4096 regresses to 0.945x.
The candidate uses a cuBLAS plus handwritten deterministic rank-16 update strategy; the profile labels the bottleneck as rank16_update_or_memory_bandwidth.
The main W @ X term is a full d x d SGEMM and likely dominates total runtime, so optimizing the small rank-16 path with a custom kernel has limited upside and can introduce shape-specific overhead.
Static warnings about no dtype/contiguity validation are not performance problems under the trusted-input benchmark constraints; adding checks or copies would likely make this slower.

recommended_actions: try float4 stores when d is divisible by 4
fall back to ATen if numerical error appears
Do not promote this version as a general replacement because it regresses significantly at d=4096 despite small gains at d=3584 and d=4608.

Move toward the minimal trusted-input pure cuBLAS three-SGEMM implementation based on /workspace/lora/3_sgemm.cu: direct cuBLAS for W @ X, direct cuBLAS for B.T @ X into U, and direct cuBLAS accumulation of A @ U into Y.

Use the exact three-SGEMM mapping: SGEMM1 opA=N/opB=N with A=X and B=W to compute Y; SGEMM2 opA=N/opB=T with A=X and B=B, m=d, n=16, k=d, U shape {d,16}, ldc=d; SGEMM3 opA=N/opB=N with A=U and B=A, m=d, n=d, k=16, beta=1 to accumulate into Y.
Allocate the low-rank temporary U as shape {d,16}; avoid treating it as column-major [16,d] or using leading dimension 16.
Use CUDAGuard and at::cuda::getCurrentCUDABlasHandle, and pass tensor.data_ptr<float>() directly inside cublasSgemm calls.

Keep the implementation trusted-input and minimal: no ATen matmul, no explicit B.T materialization, no tensor validation, no input copies, no explicit d range checks, and no unnecessary stream rebinding.
If retaining this custom rank-16 kernel, only consider shape-aware dispatch after more benchmarking, because the current no-dispatch candidate is not uniformly faster.
